staged_note: Reviewer-gated exogenous sources held in staged/ (see staged/README.md). NOT scanned by the top-level *.txt globs in bench_formalize.py, cgb.py _ledger_sync, or tests/test_mathsources_manifest.py's EXPECTED_TOTAL/bijection. Promotion (move file to top level, entry to `files`, bump EXPECTED_TOTAL, re-baseline bench) is a reviewer action. `file` values are relative to staged/. Fields beyond the `files` schema (existential, binder_hint, provenance, staged_reason) are staging-only metadata.

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b existential witness: Archimedean-type bounded/finitizable exists (n) over ordering and multiplication -- a genuine exists binder distinct from the additive unboundedness of 43.

provenance.reference: Archimedean property (ordered field)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikipedia)
provenance.verbatim: for any two positive real numbers a and b, there exists a positive integer n such that na > b
provenance.rendering_note: specialized to a a positive integer and b any integer (Nat/Int fragment; no reals, no abs); 'na' rendered 'n times a'.

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b existential witness: existence of an additive inverse (n + m = 0); flat exists over integer addition.

provenance.reference: Integers under Addition form Abelian Group (inverse element axiom)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: each element z of (Z, +) has an inverse element -z: z + (-z) = 0
provenance.rendering_note: the additive-inverse axiom rendered as an explicit existential 'there exists m such that n plus m equals 0'.

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b existential witness: existence of a common multiple (a | m and b | m); exercises divisibility under an existential. The product a*b is a witness (finitizable).

provenance.reference: Least common multiple (existence of a common multiple)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikipedia)
provenance.verbatim: the lowest common multiple ... is the smallest positive integer that is divisible by both a and b
provenance.rendering_note: existence half only -- 'divisible by both a and b' rendered 'a divides m and b divides m'; 'smallest' dropped (no unique-min in fragment); specialized to positive a, b.

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b existential witness: solvability of a + x = b (Latin square property of the additive group); over Nat the witness need not exist (b < a) -- a genuine Nat-vs-Int flip recorded as ambient-ambiguity.

provenance.reference: Group has Latin Square Property (unique solution of a x = b), applied to (Z, +)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: there exists a unique x in G such that a x = b
provenance.rendering_note: applied to the additive group (Z, +): existence of x with a + x = b; uniqueness dropped (no unique-exists in fragment).

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b existential witness: parity dichotomy with explicit witness k (n = 2k or 2k+1); flat exists over multiplication/addition in corpus-native parity vocabulary.

provenance.reference: Even and Odd Integers form Partition of Integers
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: The set of even integers and the set of odd integers form a partition of the set of integers (every integer is even or odd, and no integer is both).
provenance.rendering_note: existence half of the partition: every integer is even or odd; unfolds Definition:Even Integer (n = 2k) and Definition:Odd Integer (n = 2k + 1) into the explicit witness k, giving 'n equals 2 times k or n equals 2 times k plus 1' -- a genuine existential over k.
provenance.verified_via: WP-SRC2 reviewer web-search verification (direct fetch org-blocked); re-cited from the prior 'Integer is Even or Odd' title, which could not be verified to exist on ProofWiki -- this partition page is the verifiable canonical CC BY-SA source stating the dichotomy.

provenance.reference: Definition:Congruence (Number Theory)/Integers
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: a is congruent to b modulo m if and only if m divides a - b
provenance.rendering_note: forward direction only (congruence implies m | (a - b)); side condition m a positive integer; 'a - b' rendered 'the difference a minus b'.

provenance.reference: Integer Divisor Results/Integer Divides Zero
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: n divides 0 (every integer divides zero)
provenance.rendering_note: since 0 = n times 0; rendered 'n divides 0'.

provenance.reference: Greatest common divisor is commutative
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikipedia)
provenance.verbatim: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a)
provenance.rendering_note: 'gcd(a,b)' rendered 'the greatest common divisor of a and b'.

provenance.reference: GCD with Zero
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: gcd(a, 0) = |a|
provenance.rendering_note: specialized to nonnegative a so |a| = a; 'gcd(a,0)' rendered 'the greatest common divisor of a and 0'.

provenance.reference: Square Modulo 4
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: x is even iff x^2 == 0 (mod 4); x is odd iff x^2 == 1 (mod 4)
provenance.rendering_note: the disjunctive corollary -- for every integer n, n times n is congruent to 0 or 1 modulo 4; drops the even/odd biconditional refinement.

provenance.reference: Definition:Greatest Common Divisor (the gcd is a common divisor)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (ProofWiki)
provenance.verbatim: Let d = gcd(a, b). Then d divides a and d divides b
provenance.rendering_note: the common-divisor half of the gcd definition (gcd(a,b) divides a).

holdout_note: WP-HOLDOUT (COMPRESSION.md 11.7): a contamination-resistant held-out exogenous set (~20 readings) for cost measurement (WP-MET) and out-of-sample generalization claims. Sources are held in holdout/ and listed here in the separate `holdout` array (append-only keys, NOT `files`), mirroring the staged/ quarantine convention. NOT scanned by any top-level *.txt glob (bench_formalize.py _CORPUS.glob, cgb.py _ledger_sync ms.glob, demo_ledger.py, tests/test_mathsources_manifest.py bijection) -- all of those are top-level-only, so holdout/ is INERT with respect to bench denominators, the ledger, and the pinned manifest test. `file` values are relative to holdout/. Selection is by a NEUTRAL rule (sequential canonical order over Euclid's Elements), documented as binding in holdout/README.md; each entry carries a `curation` block recording the neutral-rule fields and a `provenance` block (reference/url/license/verbatim/rendering_note/verified_via, matching the citation-fix convention).

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book VII, Definition 6 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: An even number is that which is divisible into two equal parts.
provenance.rendering_note: 'divisible into two equal parts' rendered as the existential n = k + k (two equal parts each of size k); 'even' is the lexicon predicate.
provenance.verified_via: snippet-concordant, 2026-07-17: mathcs.clarku.edu (D. E. Joyce edition of T. L. Heath's translation) is blocked for direct fetch by the session egress policy (403 to WebFetch); the statement text was returned concordantly by clarku.edu-domain-restricted web search. Joyce's edition lightly modernizes Heath's 1908 wording (e.g. 'are added together' for Heath's 'be added together'); the verbatim quoted here is the Joyce-edition wording as returned. Not verified by direct page fetch.

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book VII, Definition 11 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: A prime number is that which is measured by a unit alone.
provenance.rendering_note: the fragment has no `prime` operator (MATH_OPERATORS); marked non-transcribable to match the repo's documented boundary (38_infinitude_primes / 51_goldbach carry the same operator:prime miss). Honest caveat: the definitional content unfolds to a universal-over-divisors ('for all d, d divides n implies d = 1 or d = n'), which IS in-fragment, but that unfold is not the fragment's intended vocabulary and is not pursued (no workaround-hunting).

provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: Numbers prime to one another are those which are measured by a unit alone as a common measure.
provenance.rendering_note: 'measured by a unit alone as a common measure' rendered as gcd(a, b) = 1 using the lexicon `gcd`/`coprime` (Nat-only); positivity is carried as a presupposition.

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book VII, Definition 13 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: A composite number is that which is measured by some number.
provenance.rendering_note: no `composite` operator in the fragment; marked non-transcribable on the same footing as prime (a named unary predicate absent from MATH_OPERATORS). Honest caveat: the content ('exists d, 1 < d < n and d divides n') is expressible via exists+dvd, but I defer to the prime precedent rather than invent a boundary crossing. Contrast h09 (composite-to-one-another), whose content is a plain binary common-divisor existential the corpus already expresses, hence taken as transcribable.
provenance.verified_via: snippet-concordant, 2026-07-17: mathcs.clarku.edu (D. E. Joyce edition of T. L. provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book VII, Definition 14 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: Numbers composite to one another are those which are measured by some number as a common measure.
provenance.rendering_note: 'measured by some number as a common measure' rendered as the existential common divisor: exists d > 1 with d | a and d | b. The 'some number' is a non-unit by Euclid's own unit-vs-number contrast (Def 12 unit-alone vs Def 14 some-number). No absent operator is needed -- this is the negation of coprimality stated directly.

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book VII, Definition 22 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: A perfect number is that which is equal to its own parts.
provenance.rendering_note: requires the SUM of all proper divisors -- an unbounded aggregate the fragment cannot form (no divisor-sum operator, and the sum ranges over an a-priori-unknown set of divisors). The strongest boundary of the three non-transcribables here: unlike prime/composite there is no quantifier unfold either.

provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: If as many even numbers as we please are added together, then the sum is even.
provenance.rendering_note: Euclid's unbounded 'as many as we please' is specialized to the two-summand case (the corpus's binary-sum convention, cf. 04_even_plus_even); the fragment's `+` is variadic so wider renderings are also in-fragment.

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book IX, Proposition 27 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: If an even number is subtracted from an odd number, then the remainder is odd.
provenance.rendering_note: ambient Int (see h16).

provenance.reference: Euclid, Elements, Book IX, Proposition 28 (Heath translation; David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.license: Public Domain (T. L. Heath translation of Euclid's Elements, 1908; presented in the David E. Joyce edition, Clark University)
provenance.verbatim: If an odd number is multiplied by an even number, then the product is even.
provenance.rendering_note: direct: odd(a) and even(b) imply even(a*b). Quota of 20 in-fragment sources is reached here; the walk stops (IX.29-36 recorded but not transcribed).
